input_row.title: Ensure server parameter 'log_disconnections' is set to 'ON' for PostgreSQL Database Server
input_row.rationale: Enabling log_disconnections helps PostgreSQL Database to Logs end of a session, including duration, which in turn generates query and error logs. Query and error logs can be used to identify, troubleshoot, and repair configuration errors and sub- optimal performance. Impact: Enabling this setting will enable a log of all disconnections. If this is enabled for a high traffic server, the log may grow exponentially.
input_row.audit: From Azure Portal 1. From Azure Home select the Portal Menu 2. Go to Azure Database for PostgreSQL servers 3. For each database, click on Server parameters 4. Search for log_disconnections. 5. Ensure that value is set to ON. From Azure CLI Ensure log_disconnections value is set to ON az postgres server configuration show --resource-group <resourceGroupName> -- server-name <serverName> --name log_disconnections  From PowerShell Ensure log_disconnections value is set to ON Get-AzPostgreSqlConfiguration -ResourceGroupName <ResourceGroupName> - ServerName <ServerName> -Name log_disconnections  From Azure Policy If referencing a digital copy of this Benchmark, clicking a Policy ID will open a link to the associated Policy definition in Azure. If referencing a printed copy, you can search Policy IDs from this URL: https://portal.azure.com/#view/Microsoft_Azure_Policy/PolicyMenuBlade/~/Definitions • Policy ID: eb6f77b9-bd53-4e35-a23d-7f65d5f0e446 - Name: 'Disconnections should be logged for PostgreSQL database servers.'
input_row.remediation: From Azure Portal 1. From Azure Home select the Portal Menu 2. Go to Azure Database for PostgreSQL servers 3. For each database, click on Server parameters 4. Search for log_disconnections. 5. Click ON and save. From Azure CLI Use the below command to update log_disconnections configuration. az postgres server configuration set --resource-group <resourceGroupName> -- server-name <serverName> --name log_disconnections --value on From PowerShell Use the below command to update log_disconnections configuration. Update-AzPostgreSqlConfiguration -ResourceGroupName <ResourceGroupName> - ServerName <ServerName> -Name log_disconnections -Value on Default Value: By default log_disconnections is disabled (set to off). References: 1. https://docs.microsoft.com/en- us/rest/api/postgresql/singleserver/configurations/list-by-server 2. https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/postgresql/howto-configure-server- parameters-using-portal 3. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/mcsb-logging-threat- detection#lt-3-enable-logging-for-security-investigation 4. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/module/az.postgresql/get- azpostgresqlconfiguration?view=azps-9.2.0#example-2-get-specified-postgresql- configuration-by-name 5. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/module/az.postgresql/update- azpostgresqlconfiguration?view=azps-9.2.0#example-1-update-postgresql- configuration-by-name
input_row.impact: Enabling this setting will enable a log of all disconnections. If this is enabled for a high traffic server, the log may grow exponentially.

gpt_response:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The audit can be performed using Azure CLI and PowerShell commands which query the server parameter directly. 
- The control is based on a standard Azure resource property that can be checked via APIs.
- Explicit commands are provided in the "audit" field, allowing confirmation through automated means.

**PROGRAM NAME:** azure_postgresql_server_log_disconnections_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:** 
The automated program will:
- Use Azure Resource Manager APIs or Azure CLI commands to check the server parameter settings for PostgreSQL.
- Enumerate PostgreSQL servers within specified resource groups or subscriptions.
- Validate the property **log_disconnections** to ensure it is set to **ON**.

**Steps to automate the checking:**
1. **Enumeration**: Identify all Azure Database for PostgreSQL servers within a target resource group or subscription. Use the command:
   ```
   az postgres server list --resource-group <resourceGroupName>
   ```
2. **Configuration Check**: For each PostgreSQL server, retrieve the configuration for **log_disconnections** using either:
   **Azure CLI**:
   ```
   az postgres server configuration show --resource-group <resourceGroupName> --server-name <serverName> --name log_disconnections
   ```
   **PowerShell**:
   ```
   Get-AzPostgreSqlConfiguration -ResourceGroupName <ResourceGroupName> -ServerName <ServerName> -Name log_disconnections
   ```
3. **Validation**: 
   - Check if the **value** returned from the above command is set to **ON**.
   - Pass/Fail criteria:
     - **Pass** if value = **on**
     - **Fail** if value = **off** (or any other value)

**Example API endpoint or CLI command**: Directly using the above CLI command will provide the necessary validation of the server parameter's compliance status.
